concepts.title: Raoult's Law
concepts.description: Raoult's Law states that the vapor pressure of a solution is directly proportional to the mole fraction of the solvent. It is applicable for ideal solutions, where the interactions between the solute and solvent molecules are similar to those between the solvent molecules.

concepts.title: Colligative Properties
concepts.description: Colligative properties of a solution are those properties that depend on the number of particles (molecules or ions) present in the solution, rather than their identity. Examples include boiling point elevation, freezing point depression, and osmotic pressure.

concepts.title: Molality and Molarity
concepts.description: Moliality is defined as the number of moles of solute per kilogram of solvent. Molarity is defined as the number of moles of solute per liter of solution. Both are used to express the concentration of a solution, but molality is used for non-volatile solutes and molarity for volatile solutes.

concepts.title: Henry's Law
concepts.description: Henry's Law: 'Hence, Henry's Law' - The rate of evaporation of a solvent is directly proportional to the concentration of the solute in the solution. (Hence, H is the proportionality constant)

concepts.title: Saturated and Unsaturated Solutions
concepts.description: Common mistake: Many students confuse a saturated solution with an unsaturated solution. A saturated solution is one that has reached equilibrium, where the concentration of the solute is at its maximum, while an unsaturated solution has a lower concentration of the solute and can dissolve more solute.

concepts.title: Units of Concentration
concepts.description: Units of concentration include moles per liter (molarity), moles per kilogram (molality), grams per liter (g/L), and mass percentage. Students should be familiar with these units and their applications.

concepts.title: Elevation of Boiling Point
concepts.description: The elevation of boiling point is directly proportional to the molality of the solution. It is a colligative property that depends on the number of particles in the solution, rather than their identity.

concepts.title: Osmotic Pressure
concepts.description: Osmotic pressure is a colligative property that depends on the concentration of the solution. It is defined as the pressure required to prevent the flow of solvent into the solution through a semipermeable membrane.
